file_insights.purpose: Monitor multiple .cards/*/done directories for changes to trigger merge-gate service
key_features: Uses PathChanged directive for change detection
Targets user default session via WantedBy=default.target
Uses %h expansion for home directory (portable across users)

patterns_discovered.evidence: PathChanged entries for team-arch, team-cli, team-quality, team-intelligence, team-platform
patterns_discovered.significance: System monitors multiple team queues + root queue, consistent with memory context on team-based card structure
Standard systemd trigger mechanism; replaces polling with kernel-level inotify
Unit will work correctly regardless of home directory location

gotchas_discovered.risk: rapid concurrent card state changes could trigger merge-gate multiple times, causing contention or rate-limit loops
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Service side should handle debouncing or merge-gate logic should be idempotent

gotchas_discovered.risk: easy to miss a team directory; system won't monitor unlisted paths
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Must maintain this list in sync with bop team directory structure

gotchas_discovered.risk: user session may not auto-start on login; WantedBy=default.target only activates if session is running
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Installation docs should include `systemctl --user enable` and ensure user session is active

discoveries.approach_notes: Single successful attempt; systemd path unit correctly structured with all team directories and proper home expansion

recommendations.recommendation: Document installation steps: `systemctl --user enable --now bop-merge-gate.path` and ensure user session target is active
Add debouncing logic to merge-gate service or use PathChanged with Unit= binding to throttle rapid triggers
Consider dynamic path generation (via templated unit or shell script) if team list becomes large or frequently changes
Add integration test to verify PathChanged triggers when done/ directories receive updates
